definitions.text: To do with a place or places. A road, a direction, a (physical or conceptual) path from one place to another.
examples: Do you know the way to the airport? Come this way and I'll show you a shortcut. It's a long way from here.
Twenty miles surely is a long way to walk.

definitions.text: A road, a direction, a (physical or conceptual) path from one place to another.
examples: Do you know the way to the airport? Come this way and I'll show you a shortcut. It's a long way from here.
Twenty miles surely is a long way to walk.

definitions.text: A means to enter or leave a place.
examples: We got into the cinema through the back way.

definitions.text: Yes; it is true; it is possible.

examples: I used to live way over there.
The farmhouse is way down the bottom of the hill.

definitions.text: Much, far, by a great degree.
examples: I'm a way better singer than Emma.
I'm way too tired to do that.
String theory is way cool, except for the math.
Sitting in the way back of the bus

definitions.text: A seventeenth-century unit of Rhenish glass containing 60 bunches.
The letter for the w sound in Pitman shorthand.
